english: rtén (rten) B. and col. a present of welcome, frq., a present in general, also a fee Glr.; p'yag-rtén rgya-čéń (phyag rten rgya chen) immense presents Glr. — p'yag-mt'íl (phyag mthil) resp. palm of the hand. — p'yag-mt'éb (phyag mtheb) resp. thumb. — p'yag-dár (phyag dar) sweepings, dust, rubbish; p'yag-dár byed-pa (phyag dar byed pa) Dzl. and elsewh., p'yag-pa (phyag pa) Lex., *gyáb-če* (*gyab ce*) W. to sweep, to clean; p'yag-dar-pa (phyag dar pa) a sweeper Dzl.; p'yag-dár-gyi p'úń-po (phyag dar gyi phung po), p'yag-dar-k'ród (phyag dar khrod) dust-heap; p'yag-dar-k'ród-kyi č'ós-gos (phyag dar khrod kyi chos gos) or náb-za (nab za) vestment or cowl of a mendicant friar, which according to the rules of his order is to be patched up of rags gathered from heaps of rubbish Burn. I, 305. (The explanation given by Sch. seems to rest on mere hypothesis.) — p'yag-na-rdó-rje (phyag na rdo rje), p'yag-rdór (phyag rdor) v. rdo-rje-č'ań (rdo rje chang). — p'yag-dpé (phyag dpe) resp. for dpé-č'a (dpe cha) v. dpe (dpe) 3. — p'yag-dpúń (phyag dpun) resp. for arm. — p'yág-p'yi (phyag phyi) attendant, man-servant = żabs-p'yi (zhabs phyi); p'yág-p'yi byéd-pa (phyag phyi byed pa) to be a servant; p'yág-p'yi-la (phyag phyi la) or p'yag-p'yír bréń-ba (phyag phyir breng ba) to be a follower (of a Lama); collect. train of servants, retinue. — p'yag-p'yís (phyag phyis) resp. towel. — p'yag-brís (phyag bris) resp. 1. hand-writing, manuscript. 2. drawing Glr. 3. letter W., brtsé-bai p'yag-brís (brtse ba'i phyag bris) your kind letter, your friendly correspondence. — p'yag-_obúl (phyag 'bul) resp. gift, present. — p'yag-sbál (phyag sbal) Cs. resp. = p'yag-goń (phyag gong); Sch. p'yag-sbál-du bcúg-pa (phyag sbal du bcug pa) to hold one's hand ready for taking or receiving, v. sbal (sbal). — p'yag-smán (phyag sman) 1. resp. for sman (sman) C. 2. = p'yag-rtén (phyag rten) W. — p'yag-ma (phyag ma) broom, duster, mop C., Lexx. — p'yag-tsáń (phyag tshang) Sch.: 'the all-filling One, the all-universalizing One' (?) — p'yag-mts'án (phyag mtshan) the attributes or emblems of Buddha and of different deities, carried in the hands (it is indeed nothing else than what, when carried in the hands of men, is called lag- (lag-) or p'yag-č'ás (phyag chas) Glr. and elsewh.). — p'yag-mdzúb (phyag mdzub) resp. for finger. — p'yag-mdzód (phyag mdzod) treasurer, of kings or in large monasteries. — p'yag-rdzás (phyag rdzas) resp. for nor-rdzás (nor rdzas) Mil. — p'yag-żábs (phyag zhabs) resp. for rkań-lág (rkang lag) Schr. — p'yag-ra (phyag ra) (prob. for p'yag-gra (phyag gra)) privy, water-closet. — p'yag-rás (phyag ras) resp. for towel Sch. — p'yag-lán (phyag lan) the return of a salutation, reciprocal greeting Mil. — p'yag-lás (phyag las) W. resp. for las (las) = p'rin-las (phrin las) B. — p'yag-lén (phyag len) resp. for lag-lén (lag len) practice, exercise, also ceremony(?) religious rite(?); . . . la-p'yag-lén _odebs-pa (la phyag len 'debs pa) Pth? . . . la-p'yag-lén-du _ogro-ba (la phyag len du 'gro ba) Mil.(?) — p'yag-śiń (phyag shing) an attribute of idols, resembling a rod (birch) or besom Wdk. — p'yág-sa (phyag sa) = p'yag-ra (phyag ra); p'yag-sén (phyag sen) resp. for sén-mo (sen mo); p'yag-sór (phyag sor) resp. for sór-mo (sor mo). — p'yag-sról (phyag srol) law, regulation; practice, use; tradition.

Cs.: = jòl-le-ba (jol le ba), hanging down (belly, v. p'yal (phyal)); Lexx. give वाम, slender, slight-made; Sch.: straight, stretched(?); p'yań-p'rúl (phyang phrul) or -_oprúl (-'phrul) Lexx. pendent ornaments.
vulg. ped-péd (ped ped), awkward gambols, clumsy attempts at dancing.

also _op'yád-pa ('phyad pa), constant, firm, persevering; p'yád-par (phyad par), always, continually, perpetually; Lexx. = rgyún-du (rgyun du) (of rare occurrence); p'yad ma p'yod (phyad ma phyod) Mil.?
= lčam (lcam) (Sbh. also kyam (kyam)), p'yam-rńas (phyam rngas), -rten (-rten), -stegs (-stegs), support (of rafters); Sch.: the resting-point of a beam.
Thgr. glittering; cf. lčám-me-ba (lcam me ba).
Glr. slow, not hasty, not greedy, indifferent to.
Sch. blame, affront, insult (v. _op'yá-ba? ('phya ba?)) p'yar-yyáń (phyar yyang) Sch. id.; Lexx. p'yar-yyéń? (phyar yyeng?)
resp. belly, stomach, Cs.; p'yal-p'yań-ńe (phyal phyang nge), Lexx. = gród-pa jòl-le-ba (grod pa jol le ba), paunch, swag-belly; p'yál-mo (phyal mo) id.?

(W. *p'i* (*phi*)) I. behind adv.: p'yi-bkan-du nyál-ba (phyi bkan du nyal ba) Sch., to lie on one's back; p'yi-gros-su gyé-ba (phyi gros su gye ba), Glr.; *č'i-ḍo gyáb-pa* (*chi do gyab pa*) C., to retreat, to recede, with the back in advance; p'yi lús-pa (phyi lus pa), to lag behind; p'yi-rtiń (phyi rting) Sch., heel; p'yí-sdér (phyi sder), Sch. the spur of birds; p'yí-na (phyi na), Cs.; behind; p'yí-nas (phyi nas), Cs., from behind; *p'í-nur-la* (*phi nur la*) or *p'í-log-la dúl-če* (*phi log la dul ce*), to walk backward, W.; p'yi-bżin yoń (phyi bzhin yong), pursuing he comes rapidly near, Mil.; p'yi mig ltá-ba (phyi mig lta ba), to look round (back), Glr., p'yi mig ma ltá-bar (phyi mig ma lta bar), without looking round; p'yi mig čig gzigs-pas (phyi mig cig gzigs pas), resp. just looking round (back), Mil.; *p'i (mig) lóg-te ltá-če* (*phi (mig) log te lta ce*) W. id. — p'yí-p'yir (phyi phyir), behind, following, e.g. p'yi
